critique.summary: The author reflects on their transition away from Windows, noting that their work now primarily relies on Linux and Apple hardware, with Windows serving mainly as a host for Linux via WSL2. They express that Microsoft products no longer hold significant relevance in their professional or personal life.
mainPoints: The author primarily uses Apple Macs for hardware and Linux for deployment.
Windows is mainly used as a host for Linux environments through WSL2, especially for CUDA-related work.
The author no longer feels tied to Windows or Microsoft products, having moved away from them over time.
Gaming needs can often be met on Linux via tools like Steam Proton.
The author has a history of deep involvement with Microsoft technologies but now subscribes to no Microsoft products except GitHub, which they consider easily replaceable.
The author acknowledges that others may have different experiences and that Apple hardware quality influences their platform choice.
critique.truthfulness: The article is a personal reflection and opinion piece rather than a factual report. The author's statements about their own usage and preferences are credible as subjective experience. The claim that many deployments occur on Linux and that WSL2 is used for CUDA work aligns with known industry trends and technical capabilities. The mention of Steam Proton supporting gaming on Linux is accurate. However, the article does not provide external sources or data to support broader claims about platform dominance or user trends, so those should be viewed as personal perspective rather than verified fact.
considerations: The article does not address potential challenges or limitations of switching fully to Linux, such as software compatibility or enterprise support.
It overlooks the diversity of user needs and industries where Windows or Microsoft products remain dominant.
The impact of cloud ecosystems and enterprise software tied to Microsoft is not deeply examined.
The article does not consider the role of other operating systems beyond Linux and macOS.
The personal nature of the article means it may not represent broader market or user trends.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers around the viability of switching from Windows to Linux, with users sharing personal experiences and opinions on software alternatives and platform loyalty. Some express optimism about Linux's progress, while others highlight practical business concerns and comparisons to Apple.

comments.author: hootz
comments.text:
Over the last decade or so I've tried to switch to Linux a bunch of times. This will probably be my final attempt.

Almost everything is already working on Linux, I can play all the games I like, there are good open source or free proprietary alternatives for all the software I use. I truly believe we are approaching the year of Linux desktop.
comments.analysis: This comment provides a balanced and experience-based perspective on Linux usability and software availability, reflecting a realistic view of the current state of Linux desktop adoption.

comments.author: matheusmoreira
comments.text:
> Realized after writing this that I do use one Microsoft product: Github.

> It is completely fungible to me, however, and the slightest nuisance and I would just move elsewhere.

GitHub's got something its competitors don't have: GitHub Sponsors. It's a nice way to support creators and a pretty good reason to stay on GitHub. If only it was more popular.
comments.analysis: The comment accurately highlights a unique feature of GitHub that influences user retention, adding nuance to the discussion about platform dependency.

comments.author: jmclnx
comments.text:
Apple user does not like windows, color me surprised :)

But he is correct, it is time to dump windows. But for all the reasons one would have to dump windows, half or more also applies to Apple.

So yes, dump windows and move to Linux or a BSD.
comments.analysis: This comment offers a comparative critique of Windows and Apple, acknowledging shared issues while supporting the idea of moving away from Windows, though it is somewhat subjective.

comments.author: bigbuppo
comments.text: I'll believe it when I lose my last remaining customers to other platforms. We're going out of business in June.
comments.analysis: This comment reflects a personal business perspective with a pessimistic tone, providing a real-world consequence but limited broader insight into the platform debate.
